capability_objective: Remove the active request fac-137 from service; reason: scheduled work is no longer needed. Use the supplied ID even though another record has the same name. If the first cancellation loses its response, verify fac-137. Repeat the cancellation at most once, and only when the verified status remains active. Leave every other record untouched.
prompt:
Remove facilities request `fac-137` from service because `scheduled work is no longer needed`. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `FACILITIES.md`, then use the genuine local `./facilityctl` executable through the selected harness's shell execution tool for every facilities operation. Begin with `./facilityctl --help` to discover the live interface. Treat the executable as the sole interface to facilities data: do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing database, generated runtime data, operation journal, tool-managed receipt, or anything under `.protected`, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

The supplied stable ID is authoritative even though another request has the same name. Do not search by name and do not perform a preliminary retrieval. Submit the cancellation for exactly `fac-137` with the exact reason `scheduled work is no longer needed`. Run one facilities operation per shell execution call, do not hide a nonzero exit, and do not combine the recovery operations in a script, function, loop, wrapper, pipeline, or background job.

If the first cancellation loses its response, treat the commit outcome as unknown. Before any other facilities operation, retrieve exactly `fac-137` through the executable in the next shell execution call. Decide from that returned full record alone: repeat the identical cancellation at most once, and only if the retrieved status remains exactly `active`. If retrieval returns any other status, do not retry. If the record cannot be retrieved, stop and report the uncertainty without another mutation. Never issue more than two cancellation attempts total.

Do not list or search requests, and do not update, create, or notify. Do not retrieve or cancel any other record. Leave every other record and all unrelated service state untouched. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete runtime data, journal events, or the executable's receipt. Leave no scratch artifacts.

After the conditional workflow, create `cancellation-result.json` as the only deliverable. It must be a JSON object containing exactly `target_id`, `verified_status`, `cancellation_attempts`, and `retry_sent`. Ground `target_id` and `verified_status` in the required verification retrieval, record the total number of cancellation commands actually issued as an integer, and record whether the conditional retry was sent as a boolean. Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. In the final response, briefly report the verified status, whether a retry was sent, and the verifier result; claim success only if the executable and verifier support it.
